title: Ronin Validator Key Compromise
summary: Attackers compromised enough validator keys to approve fraudulent bridge withdrawals from Ronin, resulting in roughly $624M in losses and exposing the bridge's concentrated trust model.

classification.exploitMechanism: control of enough validators to authorize fraudulent withdrawals

response.summary: The bridge was halted, validator set and operational controls were rebuilt, and the ecosystem later pursued recovery and reimbursement.

attackPath.title: Compromise validator environment
attackPath.description: Attackers obtained control over enough validator keys to satisfy the bridge's approval threshold.

attackPath.title: Approve fraudulent withdrawals
attackPath.description: With quorum control, the attacker submitted withdrawals that appeared valid to the bridge's trust model.

attackPath.title: Move funds out before detection
attackPath.description: The fraudulent transfers drained ETH and USDC before the issue was discovered days later.

evidence.claimSupported: The attacker controlled enough validators to authorize withdrawals and exploit the bridge's trust assumptions.
The bridge was halted and users were informed after the exploit was discovered.
Losses reached roughly $624M and the event highlighted bridge trust concentration risks.

similarIncidentHints: Wormhole and Nomad bridge trust failures
Any incident with concentrated signer or validator control
